skill.name: zach-listing-health-checker
skill.description: Check Amazon listing health from a real consumer perspective. The skill scrapes public Amazon product pages to verify visibility, price, seller, cart, delivery, category, BSR rank, and negative reviews. It also checks keyword search visibility. Use it after a new product launch, for daily audits, or to troubleshoot listing issues.
skill.summary: Audit Amazon listings as a real shopper sees them: verify page visibility, price, seller, cart, delivery, category, BSR, and negative reviews, then confirm search visibility for target keywords.
skill.author: zach22-1999

security_audit.summary: The skill is a legitimate Amazon listing auditor that uses curl and BeautifulSoup to scrape public product and search pages, then writes a Markdown report. Most static findings are false positives caused by the heavy use of backticks in Markdown documentation and the use of subprocess to call curl with parameterized, non-user-controlled arguments. The skill is not malware: it does not exfiltrate data, does not access credentials or environment variables, and does not encode or hide behavior. The elevated risk is medium only because it performs automated scraping of a third-party service (Amazon) that may violate Amazon's Terms of Service, and uses subprocess for shell execution.

high_findings.title: Subprocess execution of curl with URL input
high_findings.description: browser_utils.py uses subprocess.run with a list-form invocation of curl. The URL is passed as a function argument, but the function is called with user-supplied ASIN or keyword values. Since arguments are passed as a list (not shell=True), this is not shell injection, but it does mean user-controlled input reaches an external HTTP client. Risk is limited to data retrieval from amazon.com domains. No exfiltration channel exists beyond the configured Amazon sites.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern matches subprocess call, but the function is a thin wrapper over curl with list-form args and a fixed UA, scoped to amazon.com domain templates. No data exfiltration, no credential access.

medium_findings.title: Automated scraping of third-party service
medium_findings.description: The skill scrapes Amazon product and search pages programmatically and may violate Amazon's Terms of Service if used at scale. The author includes a throttling function and captcha detection to reduce detection risk, but the underlying behavior is still TOS-sensitive. This is a legal/policy concern, not a security exploit.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct evidence of automated scraping of amazon.com with curl and rotating user agents. TOS risk is high; security risk is low because no malicious payload is involved.

medium_findings.title: Markdown files trigger backtick and path traversal heuristics
medium_findings.description: Static analysis flagged 60+ 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings and 8 'path traversal' findings, but on inspection all of them are inside README.md and SKILL.md documentation, not executable code. The backticks are Markdown code formatting, and '../' sequences are relative path examples in the documentation. No execution risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines confirms the flags are Markdown formatting and documentation examples, not code execution.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Amazon site domains in configuration
low_findings.description: The skill contains a hardcoded map of Amazon regional domains (amazon.com, amazon.co.uk, amazon.de, etc.) and User-Agent strings. These are configuration constants, not credentials or exfiltration endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All entries are well-known Amazon regional domains and standard browser UA strings. No suspicious endpoints.

low_findings.title: Glob pattern matching for report history
low_findings.description: run_health_check.py uses glob.glob to find previous health check reports in the output directory. The pattern is hardcoded and only reads Markdown files; no user-controlled path input is concatenated.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The glob pattern is constructed from a fixed suffix and the user-controlled --output-dir argument, but the pattern is restricted to files matching the report naming convention. No path traversal exploit path found.

dangerous_patterns.title: subprocess.run for curl invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: subprocess.run is used to call curl in browser_utils.py:62. This is a legitimate HTTP client invocation, but flagged because it executes an external binary. The URL is passed as a list element (not via shell), and no shell injection vector exists.

content.user_title: Audit Amazon listings like a real shopper
content.value_statement: Amazon sellers need a fast way to verify that their product listings render correctly to real buyers. This skill automates page-by-page checks of price, seller, cart, delivery, BSR, and negative reviews, and saves the results as a Markdown report you can share with your team.
actual_capabilities: Fetches a public Amazon product page by ASIN and extracts title, price, coupon, seller, ships-from, cart, delivery, Prime, category breadcrumb, BSR, rating, review count, and negative reviews.
Searches a keyword on Amazon and reports whether the target ASIN appears on the first page, in what position, and whether it is organic or sponsored.
Compares the current run against the most recent historical report and outputs a Markdown diff for BSR, review count, and search position.
Writes a structured Markdown health check report to a local output directory, named by date and seller.
Detects common failure states: captcha walls, dog pages, and currently-unavailable listings.
limitations: Relies on Amazon's public HTML structure. If Amazon changes their page layout, some selectors will stop matching and the affected check will report missing data.
Does not access Amazon Selling Partner API. All data comes from public frontend pages and may differ from your Seller Central view.
Does not place orders, modify listings, or perform any account-bound action.
Captcha or rate-limit responses will cause the affected ASIN or keyword to be reported as 'fetch failed' or 'captcha', not retried indefinitely.

use_cases.title: Daily listing health audit for a brand portfolio
use_cases.description: A brand owner runs the skill once per day against their full ASIN list to catch sudden price, BSR, or cart regressions before they affect revenue.
use_cases.target_user: Amazon brand managers and operations leads

use_cases.title: Post-launch verification of a new ASIN
use_cases.description: After a product goes live, a seller uses the skill to confirm the listing is visible, the correct seller name shows, the buy box is present, and the listing surfaces for target keywords.
use_cases.target_user: Amazon FBA sellers and product launch managers

use_cases.title: Negative review monitoring and report sharing
use_cases.description: An operations analyst runs the skill to surface negative first-page reviews, exports the Markdown report, and shares it with the customer service team for follow-up.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a brand with seller verification
prompt_templates.scenario: Multi-ASIN batch with seller name validation

prompt_templates.title: Full audit with keyword search visibility
prompt_templates.prompt: /zach-listing-health-checker --asins B0XXX1,B0XXX2 --seller YourBrand --keywords "your main keyword,your secondary keyword" --site US --output-dir ./reports
prompt_templates.scenario: Complete brand audit including search rank check

prompt_templates.title: Cross-marketplace audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Check the same ASIN on a different Amazon regional site

output_examples.input: ASIN B0EXAMPLE, seller MyBrand, keyword 'bluetooth speaker'
output_examples.output: Overall status: PASS. Checks completed: 8 of 8 ASIN. The report shows BSR rank, price, seller name match, cart present, Prime delivery, and 0 negative first-page reviews. Search visibility: organic position #2 for 'bluetooth speaker'.

output_examples.input: ASIN with listing removed by Amazon
output_examples.output: Overall status: FAIL. Page status: dog_page. Anomaly: '狗狗页面 - ASIN 可能已下架或不存在'. The report flags this as a high-priority issue and recommends verifying the listing in Seller Central.

best_practices: Run the skill on a fixed schedule from a stable machine (such as a Mac Mini) so the historical comparison has consistent data points.
Always pass the --seller flag for your own brand so the report can verify the Sold By field matches expectations.
Review the Markdown report directly in your editor and commit it to version control to track listing changes over time.
anti_patterns: Do not run the skill at high frequency against hundreds of ASINs at once. The throttling is intended for small batches, not bulk scraping.
Do not rely on this skill as your only source of truth for sales or revenue. It only inspects the public product page.
Do not paste the output into a public dashboard without review. The report can include negative review text, which may be sensitive.

faq.question: Does this skill access my Amazon seller account?
faq.answer: No. The skill only reads public Amazon product and search pages. It does not log in, does not use the Selling Partner API, and does not store any credentials.

faq.question: What data does the skill collect?
faq.answer: It collects publicly visible page content: title, price, seller name, cart buttons, delivery text, category breadcrumb, BSR rank, star rating, review count, and the first few first-page reviews.

faq.question: Where is the report saved?
faq.answer: The Markdown report is written to the directory you pass with --output-dir. The default is the current working directory. The filename includes the date and seller name.

faq.question: Will this get my IP blocked by Amazon?
faq.answer: The skill includes random delays between requests and User-Agent rotation. At small batch sizes (a few ASINs) this is usually safe, but Amazon may still serve captcha pages, which the skill will report honestly rather than work around.

faq.question: Can I run it on a schedule?
faq.answer: Yes. The script is a standalone Python program and can be invoked from cron, launchd, or any scheduler. No Claude Code session is required to run the actual checks.

faq.question: What if Amazon changes their page layout?
faq.answer: Some selectors may stop matching. The affected check will report the data as missing. You can update the BeautifulSoup selectors in the fetch scripts to restore coverage.
